perturbation.original_text: 1.3 “ Subsidiary ” of any entity means any other entity in which such first entity owns or Controls, directly or indirectly, an amount of the voting securities, other voting interests or voting partnership interests sufficient to elect at least a majority of such other entity’s board of directors or other governing body (or, if there are no such voting interests, 50% or more of the equity interests of such other entity). For purposes of this Section 1.3 , “Control” means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership or limited liability company, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 “ Subsidiary ” of any entity means any other entity in which such first entity owns or Controls, directly or indirectly, an amount of the voting securities, other voting interests or voting partnership interests sufficient to elect at least two-thirds of such other entity’s board of directors or other governing body (or, if there are no such voting interests, 50% or more of the equity interests of such other entity). For purposes of this Section 1.3 , “Control” means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership or limited liability company, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Subsidiary' is modified, raising the threshold for board election control from 'at least a majority' to 'at least two-thirds'. This directly contradicts the standard understanding of 'majority' in corporate governance and creates an in-text contradiction, because a majority is 50% + 1, and is being changed to 2/3rds. This change will cause uncertainty when determining whether an entity qualifies as a subsidiary, impacting obligations related to 'CEDC Group'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 “ Subsidiary ” of any entity means any other entity in which such first entity owns or Controls, directly or indirectly, an amount of the voting securities, other voting interests or voting partnership interests sufficient to elect at least two-thirds of such other entity’s board of directors or other governing body (or, if there are no such voting interests, 50% or more of the equity interests of such other entity). For purposes of this Section 1.3 , “Control” means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership or limited liability company, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Subsidiary', specifically raising the threshold for electing a board of directors from 'at least a majority' to 'at least two-thirds'. The contradicted text also uses the definition of 'at least two-thirds'. The perturbation explanation highlights that this change contradicts the standard understanding of 'majority' in corporate governance and will cause uncertainty, creating an in-text contradiction. While the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the original text definition was at 'at least a majority', meaning the change causes a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Business Deal, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Restructuring' with 'Business Deal' without a clear definition of 'Business Deal' creates ambiguity. The document refers to 'Restructuring' in other places and now, since the term isn't being called that anymore, creates and in-text contradiction. This is because it will be unclear whether the obligations under section 2.1 applies to those other sections that have the term 'Restructuring' within it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS the Recipient and CEDC wish to enter into discussions with respect to a potential restructuring of the Notes and Common Stock (a “ Restructuring ”). WHEREAS, CEDC, in such discussions, may disclose certain confidential information to the Recipient in order to initiate, facilitate, and/or progress such restructuring discussions (the “ Purpose ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to a 'Restructuring', and the contradicted text also refers to a 'Restructuring'. The changed text replaces 'Restructuring' with 'Business Deal'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Restructuring', and it's now unclear whether the 'Business Deal' includes the Restructuring. This follows the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 The Recipient shall ensure that each of its Representatives who are either provided with Confidential Information, or otherwise have access to such Confidential Information, are informed of its confidential nature and are directed to abide by the terms of this Agreement applicable to Representatives or appropriate duties or obligations of confidentiality imposing confidentiality obligations on such Representatives (except that there shall be no requirement to so inform where the Representative to whom the information is to be disclosed is subject to professional obligations to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information or is otherwise bound by requirements of confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information). The Recipient agrees to be liable for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its affiliates or Representatives. The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to provide written notice to CEDC of any use or disclosure of Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement of which Recipient is aware, including without limitation written details regarding the circumstances and the identity of the individuals or entities who as a result received or may have received access to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 The Recipient shall ensure that each of its Agents who are either provided with Confidential Information, or otherwise have access to such Confidential Information, are informed of its confidential nature and are directed to abide by the terms of this Agreement applicable to Agents or appropriate duties or obligations of confidentiality imposing confidentiality obligations on such Agents (except that there shall be no requirement to so inform where the Agent to whom the information is to be disclosed is subject to professional obligations to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information or is otherwise bound by requirements of confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information). The Recipient agrees to be liable for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its affiliates or Agents. The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to provide written notice to CEDC of any use or disclosure of Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement of which Recipient is aware, including without limitation written details regarding the circumstances and the identity of the individuals or entities who as a result received or may have received access to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Representatives' with 'Agents' introduces a potential inconsistency. While 'Representatives' is defined in Section 1.1, 'Agents' is not, potentially narrowing the scope of individuals bound by confidentiality. Since the original document defined 'Representatives' as (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants) (collectively, “ Representatives ”). And now replacing it with 'Agents' will contradict the agreement, due to the undefined nature of 'Agents'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Subject to Section 1.2 below, “ Confidential Information ” means confidential, secret, proprietary or other non-public information pertaining to the business, operations, brands, marketing plans, financial matters, legal matters, products, projects, business plans or practices, research and development, product development, intellectual property, financial models, trademarks, trade secrets, accounting and financing data, and methods of production, distribution or procurement, suppliers, distributors, consultants, advisors or employees, directors or officers of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries (defined below) (together, the “ CEDC Group ”) that is disclosed or otherwise made available, either orally or in writing, by any member of the CEDC Group to the Recipient or its affiliates, agents, or advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants) (collectively, “ Representatives ”).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Agents' introduces a potential contradiction. 'Representatives' is a defined term in Section 1.1, explicitly including 'financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants.' By replacing 'Representatives' with the undefined term 'Agents' in Section 2.4, the modification creates ambiguity and potentially narrows the scope of individuals bound by confidentiality, thus creating a contradiction with the comprehensive definition provided in Section 1.1. The original text ensures a broad scope of individuals are covered, while the changed text introduces uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The parties hereto are independent of one another and this Agreement does not create any agency, partnership or similar relationship between the parties hereto. CEDC acknowledges that neither the Recipient nor any of its affiliates, nor its or their Representatives, will be deemed to have made any representation or warranty or commitment with respect to the Purpose except as may be set forth in one or more final, legally binding definitive agreements. Except as otherwise provided by CEDC, the Recipient (i) acknowledges that neither CEDC nor any of its Representatives make any representation or warranty hereunder, either express or implied, as to the truth, accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information, provided, however , that CEDC, the CEDC Group and any of their Representatives shall use good faith efforts to ensure that all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient hereunder is true, accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge, and (ii) agrees, to the fullest extent permitted by law that in the absence of fraud or willful misconduct on their part neither CEDC nor any of its Subsidiaries or Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or its Representatives on any basis (including, without limitation, in contract, tort, under federal or state securities laws or otherwise) as a result of the review by the Recipient or its Representatives or the use of the Confidential Information by the Recipient or its Representatives in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The parties hereto are independent of one another and this Agreement does not create any agency, partnership or similar relationship between the parties hereto. CEDC acknowledges that neither the Recipient nor any of its affiliates, nor its or their Advisors, will be deemed to have made any representation or warranty or commitment with respect to the Purpose except as may be set forth in one or more final, legally binding definitive agreements. Except as otherwise provided by CEDC, the Recipient (i) acknowledges that neither CEDC nor any of its Advisors make any representation or warranty hereunder, either express or implied, as to the truth, accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information, provided, however , that CEDC, the CEDC Group and any of their Advisors shall use good faith efforts to ensure that all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient hereunder is true, accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge, and (ii) agrees, to the fullest extent permitted by law that in the absence of fraud or willful misconduct on their part neither CEDC nor any of its Subsidiaries or Advisors shall have any liability to the Recipient or its Advisors on any basis (including, without limitation, in contract, tort, under federal or state securities laws or otherwise) as a result of the review by the Recipient or its Advisors or the use of the Confidential Information by the Recipient or its Advisors in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Advisors' in this section again, further muddies the waters about who is bound by the agreement. Advisors is not defined, while representatives is defined as '(including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants)'. This will make it harder to determine if all of the parties are bound by confidentiality
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Subject to Section 1.2 below, “ Confidential Information ” means confidential, secret, proprietary or other non-public information pertaining to the business, operations, brands, marketing plans, financial matters, legal matters, products, projects, business plans or practices, research and development, product development, intellectual property, financial models, trademarks, trade secrets, accounting and financing data, and methods of production, distribution or procurement, suppliers, distributors, consultants, advisors or employees, directors or officers of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries (defined below) (together, the “ CEDC Group ”) that is disclosed or otherwise made available, either orally or in writing, by any member of the CEDC Group to the Recipient or its affiliates, agents, or advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants) (collectively, “ Representatives ”).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Advisors' creates a misaligned terminology issue, because 'Representatives' is defined in Section 1.1 (the contradicted text) to include a specific list of parties, while 'Advisors' is not explicitly defined anywhere in the contract. Changing the term to 'Advisors' in Section 3.1 now creates ambiguity as to who is bound by the agreement, particularly regarding confidentiality, which contradicts the clear definition of 'Representatives' in Section 1.1. This makes it difficult to determine if all relevant parties are bound by confidentiality as intended by the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Securities Laws. The Recipient acknowledges that it is aware (and, if applicable, that its Representatives who are apprised of this matter have been advised) that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees that, to the extent that it has actually received Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement that constitutes material non-public information, it will not trade any securities of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries prior to the termination of this Agreement and that it will not use any Confidential Information in contravention of the United States securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Securities Laws. The Recipient acknowledges that it is aware (and, if applicable, that its Associates who are apprised of this matter have been advised) that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees that, to the extent that it has actually received Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement that constitutes material non-public information, it will not trade any securities of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries prior to the termination of this Agreement and that it will not use any Confidential Information in contravention of the United States securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Associates' introduces a potential inconsistency. Since 'Associates' is not defined within the document but Representatives is defined in section 1.1, potentially narrowing the scope of individuals bound by the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 1.1 defines 'Representatives' and in the modified term this definition will not be applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Subject to Section 1.2 below, “ Confidential Information ” means confidential, secret, proprietary or other non-public information pertaining to the business, operations, brands, marketing plans, financial matters, legal matters, products, projects, business plans or practices, research and development, product development, intellectual property, financial models, trademarks, trade secrets, accounting and financing data, and methods of production, distribution or procurement, suppliers, distributors, consultants, advisors or employees, directors or officers of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries (defined below) (together, the “ CEDC Group ”) that is disclosed or otherwise made available, either orally or in writing, by any member of the CEDC Group to the Recipient or its affiliates, agents, or advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants) (collectively, “ Representatives ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Representatives' which is a defined term in section 1.1, encompassing a specific group of individuals. The changed text replaces 'Representatives' with 'Associates', a term not defined in the document. This creates a contradiction because the obligations imposed on 'Representatives' in section 6 no longer automatically apply to the individuals previously covered under the defined term in section 1.1, potentially narrowing the scope of individuals bound by the agreement, which contradicts the original intent and the existing definition in section 1.1.
